title: The Soul Should Always Stand Ajar,
content:
The soul should always stand ajar,
    That if the heaven inquire,
    He will not be obliged to wait,
    Or shy of troubling her.

    Depart, before the host has slid
    The bolt upon the door,
    To seek for the accomplished guest, --
    Her visitor no more.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emily Elizabeth Dickinson delivers a powerful performance in "The Soul Should Always Stand Ajar,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
